description: MVP: rules engine + cards + decks + API-first backbone + Supabase event log

userStories.title: Establish Python project scaffold with tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a runnable FastAPI skeleton and a working test runner so future stories have backpressure.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a minimal Python project layout for app code and tests
Add a minimal FastAPI app with a health endpoint
Add pytest and at least one passing test
Document the canonical test command in AGENTS.md
userStories.notes: Keep this story small; avoid adding Supabase integration yet.

userStories.title: Define canonical game state + card models
userStories.description: As a developer, I want JSON-serializable state/models so the reducer and event log have a stable shape.
acceptanceCriteria: Add data models for card definitions and game snapshot state
Models are JSON-serializable and stable
Unit tests cover basic model validation (side ranges, required fields)
userStories.notes: Prefer pydantic.

userStories.title: Implement capture resolution for a placement
userStories.description: As a player, when I place a card, adjacent enemy cards flip if my touching side is higher.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement placement + capture resolution (no combos)
Unit tests cover N/E/S/W comparisons and ownership flips
Deterministic outcomes for a given input state
userStories.notes: This should live in the pure rules engine reducer; no DB/API yet.

userStories.title: Add Mists randomness modifier
userStories.description: As a player, each placement rolls 1d6 and applies -1/+1 to comparisons for that placement only.
acceptanceCriteria: Fog (1) applies -1 to all comparisons for the placement
Omen (6) applies +1 to all comparisons for the placement
2-5 apply no modifier
Unit tests cover all roll cases using a seeded RNG
userStories.notes: Modifier affects comparisons only; printed stats do not change.

userStories.title: Add turn flow and move validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the reducer to enforce turns, hands, and board placement rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Reducer rejects: wrong player turn, card not in hand, and occupied cell
Reducer advances turn after a legal placement
Unit tests cover validation errors and successful placements
userStories.notes: Still no archetypes yet; focus on core turn invariants.

userStories.title: Implement archetype power: Martial rotation
userStories.description: As a Martial player, I can rotate the placed card once before comparisons, once per game.
acceptanceCriteria: Applying Martial rotates the placed card once (N->E->S->W->N) for this placement only
Power can be used at most once per player per game
Unit tests cover rotation behavior and once-per-game enforcement
userStories.notes: Default: rotation occurs after placement selection and before Mists/captures are evaluated.

userStories.title: Implement archetype power: Skulker side boost
userStories.description: As a Skulker player, I can add +2 to one chosen side for this placement only, once per game.
acceptanceCriteria: Skulker chooses one side (n/e/s/w) and gets +2 only for comparisons this placement
Power can be used at most once per player per game
Unit tests cover side selection, effect scope, and once-per-game enforcement
userStories.notes: Choice is made at placement time (not after seeing results).

userStories.title: Implement archetype power: Caster reroll
userStories.description: As a Caster player, I can reroll the Mists die for my placement, once per game.
acceptanceCriteria: Caster rerolls the Mists die; the second result is used
Power can be used at most once per player per game
Unit tests cover reroll behavior and once-per-game enforcement
userStories.notes: Reroll applies only to Mists and only on that placement.

userStories.title: Implement archetype power: Devout fog negation
userStories.description: As a Devout player, I treat Fog (1) as no effect for my placement, once per game.
acceptanceCriteria: If Mists roll is Fog (1), Devout treats it as no modifier for that placement
If roll is not Fog, Devout does nothing
Power can be used at most once per player per game
Unit tests cover Fog negation and once-per-game enforcement
userStories.notes: Devout does not turn Fog into Omen; it neutralizes the -1.

userStories.title: Implement archetype power: Presence single-comparison boost
userStories.description: As a Presence player, I can force one adjacent comparison to be re-evaluated with +1 for me, once per game.
acceptanceCriteria: Presence chooses one adjacent direction and gets +1 only for that single comparison this placement
Power can be used at most once per player per game
Unit tests cover the single-comparison scope and once-per-game enforcement
userStories.notes: Default: applied as +1 to placed side value for the chosen comparison before evaluating capture.

userStories.title: Implement end-of-round scoring
userStories.description: As a player, when the board is full the game determines the winner by controlled card count.
acceptanceCriteria: When board is full (9 placements), compute each player's controlled count
Set a terminal result when one player has more
Unit tests cover win/lose outcomes and tie detection
userStories.notes: Sudden Death comes in the next story.

userStories.title: Implement Sudden Death rounds with cap
userStories.description: As a player, if the game is tied at board-full, it starts Sudden Death using the 9 cards each player owned.
acceptanceCriteria: On tie at board-full, increment round_number and sudden_death_rounds_used
Rebuild each player's next-round deck from the 9 cards they ended owning
Reset board and hands for the new round
After 3 sudden-death rounds, if still tied, set result to draw
Unit tests cover the transition, card carryover, and cap behavior
userStories.notes: Same game_id; round_number increments; cap=3 then draw.

userStories.title: Add cards.jsonl validator: schema + key uniqueness
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to validate cards.jsonl inputs before importing so the card pool is trustworthy.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement a cards.jsonl loader that parses JSON Lines
Validate required fields/types and card_key uniqueness
Validation errors include line numbers and human-readable messages
Unit tests cover schema failures and duplicate card_key
userStories.notes: Budget/cap enforcement is in a separate story to keep scope small.

userStories.title: Add cards.jsonl validator: rarity buckets + budget/cap enforcement
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to enforce per-tier rarity budgets so cards are balanced per CARDS_SPEC.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Derive rarity bucket from numeric rarity per CARDS_SPEC.md
Enforce side values 1..10, tier in {1,2,3}, rarity 1..100
Enforce (tier,bucket) sum budgets and per-side caps
Unit tests cover bucket boundaries and budget/cap failures
userStories.notes: Keep bucket and budget tables centralized and test-covered.

userStories.title: Implement deck validator: named uniqueness + rarity slots
userStories.description: As a developer, I want deck validation hooks to enforce anti-stacking rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Validate 10-card deck size
Enforce named uniqueness by character_key when is_named is true
Enforce rarity slots: ultra<=1, very_rare<=2, rare<=3
Unit tests cover each rule failure
userStories.notes: Copy limits are a separate story.

userStories.title: Implement deck validator: copy limits by rarity bucket
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to limit duplicates by exact card version to prevent stacked decks.
acceptanceCriteria: Enforce copy limits by card_key via a bucket-driven lookup table
Unit tests cover copy limit violations across buckets
Deck validator returns actionable error messages
userStories.notes: Default: common/uncommon allow 2 copies; higher buckets allow 1 (adjustable lookup).

userStories.title: Implement weighted deck cost + fairness matching
userStories.description: As a player, server-generated decks should be roughly even strength.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement a weighted-sum deck cost function based on tier and rarity bucket
Unit tests cover cost ordering (stronger bucket/tier => higher cost)
userStories.notes: Keep it simple and deterministic; no simulation.

userStories.title: Implement seeded deck generation producing two matched legal decks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want game creation to generate decks from the card pool reproducibly.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a card pool and seed, generate two 10-card decks that pass validation
Ensure deck costs are within a configured tolerance
Generation is deterministic given the same seed
Unit tests cover determinism and tolerance matching
userStories.notes: Store seed in game state; use it for Mists rolls too (derive per-move RNG).

userStories.title: Define GameStore/CardStore interfaces + in-memory GameStore
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a thin storage boundary so we can test the API without Supabase.
acceptanceCriteria: Define GameStore and CardStore interfaces used by FastAPI
Implement an in-memory GameStore with append-only events, per-game seq, and state_version
Integration tests use the in-memory store and validate optimistic locking
userStories.notes: Reducer must be the only rules implementation.

userStories.title: Implement FastAPI endpoints with in-memory store
userStories.description: As a developer, I want API-first iteration on the full match flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Add endpoints: POST /games, POST /games/{id}/join, POST /games/{id}/archetype, GET /games/{id}, POST /games/{id}/moves
Integration tests cover: create->join->select archetype->play moves
API returns 409 on state_version conflict
pytest passes
userStories.notes: Auth can be a simple test-only stub initially; tighten later.

userStories.title: Add Supabase SQL schema files for games + game_events
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the database schema captured in-repo for Supabase.
acceptanceCriteria: Add SQL files defining tables: profiles, games (with current_state jsonb, state_version), game_events (append-only with seq)
Add basic RLS policy stubs matching TECH_DECISIONS.md constraints
Add a small unit test that asserts the schema files exist and contain key table names
pytest passes
userStories.notes: This story captures schema in files; applying migrations can be added later.

userStories.title: Implement Supabase-backed GameStore append_event + snapshot update
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the real store to insert an event and update snapshot transactionally.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Supabase-backed GameStore implementation (service role key via env vars)
Implements insert event + update games.current_state + bump state_version
Returns 409 on optimistic lock conflict
Unit tests cover store behavior via mocks/fakes (no real Supabase credentials in repo)
userStories.notes: Keep it injectable so integration tests can still use in-memory store.

userStories.title: Add idempotency keys for move submission
userStories.description: As a player, resubmitting a move should not create duplicate events.
acceptanceCriteria: Moves accept an idempotency key
Store rejects duplicates for the same game_id + key
Integration/unit tests cover dedupe behavior
userStories.notes: Implementation can be store-specific (in-memory + Supabase).

userStories.title: Add cards seeding script for Supabase
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to load cards.jsonl into the cards table with validation.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a script that reads cards.jsonl, validates, and upserts into a cards table
Script uses env vars for Supabase connection (no secrets committed)
Unit tests cover that validation runs and that upsert calls are formed correctly (mocked)
userStories.notes: Requires defining a cards table schema in the earlier SQL story (or add it here if missing).

userStories.title: Add Supabase SQL schema files for cards table
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the cards table schema captured in-repo to match CARDS_SPEC.md and TECH_DECISIONS.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Add SQL defining a cards table with canonical columns (card_key unique, character_key, tier, rarity, is_named, n/e/s/w, set, tags) plus definition jsonb
Add constraints for side values 1..10, tier 1..3, rarity 1..100
Add a small unit test that asserts the schema file exists and contains expected columns
pytest passes
userStories.notes: Keep schema compatible with future keywords/tags expansion.

userStories.title: Standardize API error responses and status codes
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want consistent errors (403/404/409/422) so clients can handle failures reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: Return 422 on request validation errors
Return 404 when game_id does not exist
Return 403 when caller is not a game participant
Return 409 on optimistic lock conflicts
Integration tests cover these status codes
pytest passes
userStories.notes: Keep error bodies stable and minimal; avoid leaking internal details.

userStories.title: Document realtime event subscription contract
userStories.description: As a client developer, I want clear guidance on subscribing to game_events and refetching snapshots.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a short doc describing how clients subscribe to Supabase Realtime for game_events filtered by game_id
Document the event payload shape and seq ordering expectations
Document the MVP client behavior: on event, refetch GET /games/{game_id}
pytest passes
userStories.notes: This is documentation-only, but must not break tests.

userStories.title: Allow players to leave games (forfeit active, delete waiting)
userStories.description: As a player, I want to be able to leave a game. If the game is active, leaving forfeits and the other player wins. If the game is waiting with only one player, leaving deletes the game.
acceptanceCriteria: Add endpoint: POST /games/{id}/leave
If game status is ACTIVE and the caller is a participant, leaving ends the game and sets the other player as winner
If game status is WAITING and the caller is the only joined player, leaving deletes the game
For ACTIVE forfeits, append a game_forfeited event and update the snapshot transactionally
Support idempotency_key for leave (same semantics as moves)
Add store support for deleting a game with optimistic locking (in-memory + Supabase)
Unit and integration tests cover both ACTIVE and WAITING behaviors
userStories.notes: MVP: only allow forfeit when status=ACTIVE and 2 players are present; WAITING delete is only for single-player lobbies. Supabase schema already uses ON DELETE CASCADE for game_events.

userStories.title: Implement chain/combo capture resolution
userStories.description: As a player, captures can trigger additional captures (combos) for extra excitement and strategy.
acceptanceCriteria: Capture resolution supports chain/combo flips until no new captures occur
Combos propagate via newly-captured cards battling adjacent enemy cards
Mists and archetype modifiers apply only to the initially placed card's comparisons (combo captures use printed stats)
Unit tests cover a multi-step combo and modifier scoping
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: Keep it pure and deterministic; no Same/Plus or diagonal interactions.
